description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dark green and brown camouflage paint scheme, featuring two stacked wings connected by struts and wires. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, which displays a red star insignia, suggesting a military or historical context. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely accommodating a single pilot. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, characteristic of a taildragger configuration. The tail assembly is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The overall design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th-century military trainer or reconnaissance aircraft.
description: A vintage military-style biplane with camouflage paint, open cockpit, and taildragger landing gear. Features dual stacked wings, a front propeller, and a red star insignia on the tail.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the dual stacked wings, camouflage pattern, and the full span of the tail and wings.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the open cockpit, propeller, and red star insignia on the tail.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the propeller, landing gear, and the symmetrical arrangement of the wings and struts.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the tail section, wing structure, and camouflage details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine mounted at the nose with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat.
